issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on html
issuesFound.explanation: The default focus indicator is removed from the html element using scroll-behavior: smooth, and while a global :focus-visible style is defined later, the specific reset on line 185 removes the outline without a direct visible alternative for this selector.
issuesFound.fix: Line 185: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 12px; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; outline: 1px solid transparent; }

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: font-size below 16px on html
issuesFound.explanation: The root font-size is set to 12px, which is significantly below the recommended 16px (1rem) for body text, making content harder to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 188: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting word-spacing to -0.2em reduces the space between words, which can cause text to run together and significantly degrades readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 216: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting letter-spacing to -0.05em crowds characters together, making it difficult for users to distinguish individual letters and reducing overall reading speed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 217: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient line-height on paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The line-height is set to 1, which is below the WCAG AAA recommendation of 1.5 for body text, leading to cramped lines that are difficult to track.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: line-height: 1.5;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The outline is explicitly removed for elements that have focus but do not match the :focus-visible criteria, which can leave keyboard users without a focus indicator in browsers that do not fully support :focus-visible or in specific interaction scenarios.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide the element while keeping it in the accessibility tree; furthermore, the lack of clipping means it does not function as a screen-reader-only utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 381: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition to slide into view, but there is no override within a prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 425: transition: transform var(--transition-base); (fix requires adding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } })

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class has a min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum requirement of 24x24px for interactive targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 568: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class has a min-width and min-height of 20px, failing the 24x24px minimum target size requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 582: min-width: 24px; Line 583: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The outline is set to none on focus, and while a box-shadow is added, it uses a semi-transparent blue which may not provide sufficient contrast against all backgrounds or in high-contrast modes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 701: #search-input:focus { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0, 95, 204, 0.2); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary hero button uses a transform transition on hover, but no reduced-motion override is provided to stop the vertical movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 811: transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), transform var(--transition-fast); (fix requires adding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transform: none; transition: none; } })

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card uses a transform transition for a hover lift effect, but the provided prefers-reduced-motion block on line 935 only handles the hover state, not the transition itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 925: transition: border-color var(--transition-base), box-shadow var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); (fix requires adding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } })

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The product card uses a transform transition for a hover effect without a corresponding reduced-motion override to disable the transition timing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1110: transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); (fix requires adding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } })

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: The stat item uses a transform transition for a hover effect, but no reduced-motion override is provided to disable the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1205: transition: background-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); (fix requires adding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item { transition: none; } })

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card uses a transform transition for a hover effect without a reduced-motion override to disable the movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1315: transition: box-shadow var(--transition-base), border-color var(--transition-base), transform var(--transition-base); (fix requires adding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } })

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The outline is set to none on focus for form inputs, and while a box-shadow is provided, it may not be visible to all users or in high-contrast environments.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The outline is explicitly set to none on focus-visible for form elements, removing the primary keyboard focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus
issuesFound.explanation: The default browser focus indicator is removed for all elements that are not currently matching :focus-visible. If a user is using a browser or input method where :focus-visible is not triggered but :focus is, they will have no visual indication of which element has keyboard focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 284: /* Remove or provide a visible fallback for :focus */ :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }

issuesFound.title: Readability concern: font-size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The root font-size is set to 12px, which makes the base text size for the document significantly smaller than the standard 16px (1rem) recommendation. This forces users with visual impairments to zoom the page immediately to read content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting a negative word-spacing value causes words to be crowded together, which significantly degrades readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 195: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: Setting a negative letter-spacing value reduces the whitespace between individual characters, making it harder for users to distinguish between letters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 196: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses "position: static", "width: auto", and "clip: auto", which fails to visually hide the element. Furthermore, the .not-sr-only class (Line 338) and the .visually-hidden-heading class (Line 445) use properties that do not correctly hide content from sighted users while keeping it for screen readers, or they use "position: absolute" without dimensions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 311: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition to slide into view. There is no override within a prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 378: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: translateY(0); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes a transition for the 'transform' property, which triggers movement on hover or state changes. This motion is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 378: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use a transform transition to move the card 2px upwards on hover. While there is a transform: none rule on line 697, the transition itself is still active, which can cause stuttering or unwanted motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 697: .feature-card:hover { transform: none; transition: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The "Learn more" link arrow transitions its transform property on hover. This spatial movement is not guarded by a reduced motion media query.
issuesFound.fix: Line 741: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a::after { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards transition the transform property on hover. This spatial movement is not disabled in the provided reduced-motion block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 908: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card { transition: none; } .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing cards transition the transform property on hover. This spatial movement is not disabled in the provided reduced-motion block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1055: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion icon transitions its transform property (rotation). While there is a media query on line 1253, it only targets the transition and not the transform state itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1253: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of at least 24x24px, but also below the 44x44px recommended target size for mobile users, making it difficult to activate.

issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit dimensions of 28x28px. This is a small target for touch users and users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px

issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons are explicitly sized at 20x20px, which is below the 24px minimum requirement for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle height/width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle is sized at 28x28px in the media query. This is a critical navigation element that is too small for reliable touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .faq-item dt button::after

issuesFound.fix: Line 1253: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button::after { transition: none; transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of at least 24x24px, but also below the 44x44px recommended target size for mobile users, making it difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] height/width 28px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm height 20px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon height/width 20px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle height/width 28px

issuesFound.fix: Line 1355: min-height: 44px; Line 1356: min-width: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.3:1

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #ee9999 / background #ffffff ratio 2.4:1

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle CSS classes and manage scroll locking but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation drawer has changed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');` Line 1559: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer elements but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger. Assistive technologies may not perceive the button as a toggle until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1621: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function updates the `aria-pressed` attribute for all buttons in the group, but the `_onTabClick` function for product category filters only toggles a CSS class. Users of assistive technology will not receive confirmation of which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: `btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true'); (and loop to set others to 'false')`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `goTo` function in the `testimonialsCarousel` module updates the slide position and CSS classes but does not use a live region to announce the new slide content or index.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2905: `_statusEl.textContent = \`Showing testimonial \${target + 1} of \${_slides.length}\`;`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module manages error message visibility and `aria-describedby` but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3215: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', result.valid ? 'false' : 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `clearField` function resets the error message and CSS classes but does not remove or reset the `aria-invalid` attribute. A field previously marked invalid will continue to be announced as such.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3275: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle CSS classes and manage scroll locking but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1115: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');` Line 1128: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer elements but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger. Assistive technologies may not recognize the button as a toggle until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1181: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function in the `productFilter` module updates the `aria-pressed` attribute on all view-toggle buttons, but the `_onTabClick` function (Line 1815) only toggles a CSS class on the category filter buttons. Filter buttons acting as toggles must reflect their state via `aria-pressed`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1824: `qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === value)));`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module correctly sets `aria-invalid="true"` via `srUtils.markInvalid` when a field is invalid, but the success branch (Line 2616) calls `srUtils.markValid`, which only removes the `aria-describedby` reference without explicitly setting `aria-invalid="false"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2616: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` function in the `srUtils` module adds an `aria-describedby` reference to the error message but fails to set the `aria-invalid="true"` attribute on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state when it receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1045: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.fix: Line 40: <button type="button" class="nav-toggle" aria-label="Open navigation menu">

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The "Solutions" <button> controls a hidden submenu (id="solutions-submenu") but lacks an aria-expanded state. Screen reader users cannot know if the submenu is currently open or closed, failing SC 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 47: <button type="button" aria-expanded="false" aria-controls="solutions-submenu">

issuesFound.title: Form control missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The email <input> with id="newsletter-email" has no associated <label>, aria-label, or aria-labelledby. Screen reader users will not know the purpose of this field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 434: <label for="newsletter-email" class="sr-only">Email address for newsletter</label>

issuesFound.title: Personal data input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The first name <input> field (name="first_name") collects personal data but lacks an autocomplete attribute. This makes it harder for users, especially those with cognitive disabilities, to auto-fill the field, failing SC 1.3.5.
issuesFound.fix: Line 456: <input type="text" id="contact-first-name" name="first_name" autocomplete="given-name" required>

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The <th> "Feature" in the table header row lacks a scope attribute. Without it, screen reader users may not correctly associate this header cell with its column data cells.
issuesFound.fix: Line 327: <th scope="col">Feature</th>

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip (h2 to h5)
issuesFound.explanation: An <h5> follows an <h2> with no intervening <h3> or <h4>. This jump of three heading levels disrupts the document outline and makes navigation difficult for screen reader users.

Connect your project

issuesFound.title: Heading level skip (h5 to h3)
issuesFound.explanation: An <h3> follows an <h5> (after the previous fix, this would be an <h3> following another <h3>, which is acceptable). However, based on the original code, there is a skip from h5 to h3. After fixing line 169 to h3, this issue is resolved. No fix needed for this line after the previous change.

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" is generic and does not describe the link's purpose out of context. Screen reader users navigating by links will not know where the link leads.

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label
issuesFound.explanation: This is the first of two <nav> elements in the document. It lacks an aria-label or aria-labelledby to distinguish it from the other navigation landmark, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 33: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation">

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus style. This removes focus indicators for all interactive elements, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 284: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This button's focus indicator is removed without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the search submit button is focused, breaking navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 525: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: These account navigation links have their focus outline removed without an alternative visible indicator. Keyboard users lose track of focus when navigating these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 550: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, and with its padding, the total interactive area is approximately 32px tall, below the recommended 44px minimum. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, which does not hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers. This breaks screen-reader-only text like skip links and form labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 307: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition that creates spatial movement, but the accompanying @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort from this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 604: transition: none;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can reduce readability, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, which compresses characters and can reduce readability, particularly for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 121: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes underlines from all links without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator. Users with colour blindness or low vision may not distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 134: text-decoration: underline;

issuesFound.title: Base font size below 16px impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, making 1rem equal to 12px. This small base font size reduces readability for all users, especially those with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none, removing the default focus indicator. No visible alternative like box-shadow or border is provided, making focus invisible for keyboard users, who rely on visual cues to navigate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 264: Change outline: none; to outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm has min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments or using touch screens may struggle to activate these small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 432: Increase min-height to at least 24px, e.g., min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon has min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the 24px minimum. This makes the icon-only button too small for reliable interaction, especially for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 440: Increase min-width and min-height to at least 24px, e.g., min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size for #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: #search-input has height: 28px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. While it meets the 24px minimum, it may still be difficult to tap accurately on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 488: Increase height to at least 44px or ensure sufficient padding (e.g., padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4);) to enlarge the interactive area.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size for search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: #site-search button[type="submit"] has width: 28px and height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. This can lead to touch errors for users with motor challenges.
issuesFound.fix: Line 499: Increase width and height to at least 44px, e.g., width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size for account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a has min-height: 24px, which is at the minimum but below the 44px recommended size. This may cause difficulty for touch users aiming at these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 515: Increase min-height to 44px or add padding to expand the clickable area, e.g., min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3);

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class sets position: static and other properties that make content visible, failing to hide it visually. Screen reader users will not receive hidden content as intended, breaking accessibility for skip links or labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: Replace with correct sr-only styles: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .btn has transitions including transform, and .btn:active applies transform: translateY(1px) for motion. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override disables this motion, which can cause discomfort for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add a media query after the .btn:active rule: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: body has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words, making text harder to read for users with visual or cognitive disabilities, such as dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 103: Remove or set word-spacing to a non-negative value, e.g., word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: All links have text-decoration: none, removing underlines. Links are distinguished only by color, which may not be perceivable by users with low vision, color blindness, or in high-glare situations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: Add underlines to links, e.g., a { text-decoration: underline; } or provide a non-color visual differentiator like a border-bottom on hover.

issuesFound.title: Root font size too small for readability
issuesFound.explanation: html { font-size: 12px; } sets the base font size to 12px, causing body text to be too small when using rem units. This reduces readability for users with visual impairments who rely on sufficient text size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 90: Increase font-size to at least 16px, e.g., font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: Line height too tight on paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: p { line-height: 1; } sets line height to 1, which is too tight for comfortable reading. Users with dyslexia or visual impairments need adequate line spacing to track text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 115: Increase line-height to at least 1.5, e.g., line-height: var(--line-height-normal);

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: .btn:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus style. Keyboard users navigating buttons will not see focus, hindering their ability to interact.
issuesFound.fix: Line 403: Add a visible focus style, e.g., outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); or box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus);

fpTitles: Line height too tight on paragraphs

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. Keyboard users will not see where focus is, breaking navigation for screen reader and keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have outline: none on focus-visible without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which button is focused, making it impossible to interact with buttons via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments or on touch devices may struggle to activate these buttons accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 440: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon selector sets min-height: 20px and min-width: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Small touch targets are difficult to tap, especially for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 450: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and visible properties, making content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible. Screen reader users may hear duplicated content, and visual users see hidden text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 270: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform and transition animations, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for it is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience motion, which can cause dizziness or seizures.
issuesFound.fix: Line 550: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses words and reduces readability. Users with dyslexia or low vision may find text harder to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 90: body { word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The a selector sets text-decoration: none globally, removing underlines from all links. Users who are colour blind or have low vision cannot distinguish links from surrounding text by colour alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 120: a { text-decoration: underline; } a:hover { text-decoration: none; }

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button (month/year) changes visual state but does not update aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive the toggle's current state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: In function _syncToggleState(period), add: if (_toggleEl) { _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false'); }

issuesFound.title: Product count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering updates the product count, the change is not announced via ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the dynamic content change, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters function, after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches), add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found.`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked invalid, aria-invalid is not set. Screen readers cannot announce the error state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) and 3.3.1 (Error Identification).
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult function, when result.valid is false, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); When valid, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile menu trigger button does not have aria-expanded set on page load. Screen reader users cannot know the initial state of the menu, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav init function, after getting _trigger and _drawer, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When billing period changes, the savings message updates visually but without ARIA live region. Screen reader users may miss the update, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: In _updateSavingsMessage function, after setting textContent, add: if (_savingsMsg && !_savingsMsg.hasAttribute('hidden')) { _savingsMsg.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite'); _savingsMsg.setAttribute('aria-atomic', 'true'); }

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle buttons missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have initial aria-expanded set. Screen reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: In register function, after panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''), add: trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle functions (open, close, toggle) do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1782: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 1790: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open/close functions (_openInstance, _closeInstance) do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive the dropdown's state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1866: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 1876: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function changes visible product cards but does not announce the filter result to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the grid has updated, degrading experience (WCAG 4.1.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2172: After `_updateEmptyState(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied.');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks fields invalid but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers won't announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 for form error indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3126: In the else block (invalid), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`. In the if block (valid), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen reader users may not know the button controls an expandable menu until after first interaction, degrading experience (WCAG 4.1.2).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1802: In mobileNav.init, after `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are registered without an initial aria-expanded state. Screen reader users cannot perceive the widgets' initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1896: In dropdownMenu.register, after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open() and close() functions toggle the menu visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's state change, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2010: In the open() function, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line 2020: In the close() function, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle does not have aria-expanded initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an initial aria-expanded state set on page load. Screen readers may report an incorrect state until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2060: In the init() function, after `_trigger` is assigned, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide panels but do not update aria-expanded on their trigger buttons. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2120: In _openInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2130: In _closeInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not have aria-expanded initialised
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have an initial aria-expanded state set on page load. Screen readers may report an incorrect state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200: In the register() function, after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button (role="switch") does not update aria-pressed when toggled. Screen reader users will not know the button's state (month/year).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: In the setPeriod() function, inside _syncToggleState(), add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button does not have aria-pressed initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle button does not have an initial aria-pressed state set on page load. Screen readers may report an incorrect state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: In the init() function, after setting up _toggleEl, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', initPeriod === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates visible product cards but does not announce the count to screen readers via a live region. Users with visual impairments may not know the content changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3500: In applyFilters(), after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates prices but does not announce the billing period change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000: In setPeriod(), after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open() and close() functions do not announce state changes to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the menu opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2010: In open(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`. Line 2020: In close(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions do not announce state changes to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the dropdown opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2120: In _openInstance(), add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');`. Line 2130: In _closeInstance(), add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');`.

issuesFound.title: View toggle buttons do not have aria-pressed initialised
issuesFound.explanation: The product view toggle buttons (grid/list) do not have an initial aria-pressed state set on page load. Screen readers may report an incorrect state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3600: In the productFilter init() function, after setting the initial view mode, add `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(btn => setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode ? 'true' : 'false'));`.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner is shown but not announced to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the banner is present.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11000: In _showBanner(), after the banner is displayed, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.bannerMessage);`.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent decision not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent accept and decline actions do not announce the decision to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know their choice was recorded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11050: In _onAccept(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved);`. Line 11060: In _onDecline(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved);`.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown navigation trigger button in NavDropdown has aria-controls but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the submenu is currently open. Screen reader users need this state information to understand the current disclosure state.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: FormGroup component missing errorId prop usage
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component accepts errorId and errorMessage props but does not pass errorId to the ErrorMessage component. This breaks the association between form inputs and their error messages for screen reader users, preventing proper error announcement.

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The same mobileNav module never links the toggle button to the navigation drawer via aria‑controls, so screen readers cannot navigate to the controlled region.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons lack aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu, the _openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle CSS classes but never update aria‑expanded on the <button> that opens the submenu. This prevents AT users from knowing the submenu state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: faqRegistry.parse creates the trigger button and sets aria‑controls and aria‑labelledby, but never sets aria‑expanded to reflect the open/closed state. The faqAccordion open/close functions also do not update this attribute.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not reflect state with aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons (e.g., [data-period="month"] etc.) are toggles but the code only adds/removes a CSS .active class. No aria‑pressed or aria‑selected is set, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which billing period is active.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing toggle (data‑billing-toggle) missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The “monthly/annual” switch is a custom toggle that adds a CSS .checked class but never updates aria‑pressed, leaving the state invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher buttons do not expose state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: themeSwitcher.toggle changes the theme but the UI buttons (identified by [data-theme-toggle]) never receive an aria‑pressed state, so users cannot know which theme is active.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑controls linking to the price panel
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons control the visibility of price values in each plan card, but no aria‑controls attribute points to the affected region, making it hard for AT to associate the control with its result.

fpTitles: Theme‑switcher buttons do not expose state via aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded when opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button (SELECTORS.navToggle) is used to open and close the mobile navigation drawer, but the open() and close() functions never set or update aria‑expanded on the trigger element, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open/close dropdown panels (selected via SELECTORS.navDropdownTrigger) toggle the panel visibility, but the code never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons missing initial aria‑expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers are rendered with aria‑haspopup but without an initial aria‑expanded state, so screen readers cannot know they are collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
In register() add:  
```js
trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers do not update aria‑expanded when toggled
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items use [data-faq-trigger] buttons to show/hide answers, but open() and close() functions never modify the aria‑expanded attribute on the trigger, violating the ARIA accordion pattern.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing initial aria‑expanded="false" on page load
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers are given aria‑controls and role="button" but lack an initial aria‑expanded value, so the collapsed state is not conveyed.
issuesFound.fix:
In faqRegistry.parse() add after creating trigger:  
```js
trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle element does not update aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded when switched
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (SELECTORS.billingToggle) changes the displayed price but never updates aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded, leaving assistive technology users unaware of the current period.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle missing initial aria‑pressed state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle element is given role="switch” but no aria‑pressed value is set initially, so its state is ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In init() after locating _toggleEl, add:  
```js
_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid on the newsletter email input
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator marks errors via srUtils.markInvalid, which only adds aria‑describedby and does not set aria‑invalid, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid on contact form “Full name” input
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 9; the bound input never receives aria‑invalid when invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 9 (ensure markInvalid sets aria‑invalid).

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid on contact form “Email address” input
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 9.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid on contact form “Subject” input
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 9.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid on contact form “Message” textarea
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 9.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid on contact form “Consent” checkbox
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 9.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The “hamburger” button (`_trigger`) opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer, but the script never sets `aria‑expanded` on the button. Assistive technologies cannot know whether the menu is currently expanded, violating WCAG 2.2 (ARIA state management).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1029: `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` →  
    `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`  
    Line 1055: `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` →  
    `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Site‑search combobox does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`_inputEl`) shows and hides the suggestion list, but the script never toggles `aria‑expanded`. Without this, the combobox’s state is ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2103 (inside `_open`): after `suggestionListRenderer.show();` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`  
    Line 2120 (inside `_close`): after `suggestionListRenderer.hide();` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tabs missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tabs are implemented as `<button>` elements with visual “active” styling, but the script never reflects the selected state via `aria‑pressed`. This prevents screen‑reader users from knowing which filter is active.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are expanded/collapsed via `open(item)` and `close(item)`, but the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never updated. This violates ARIA state requirements for accordions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3068 (inside `open`): after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`  
    Line 3094 (inside `close`): after `removeClass(item.el, CSS.open);` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` marks fields as valid/invalid visually, but never sets the required `aria‑invalid` attribute. Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel lacks proper ARIA role and name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel container (`_rootEl`) is created with no ARIA role or accessible name, and the slides lack a `aria-roledescription`. Screen‑reader users cannot identify the component as a carousel.

fpTitles: Testimonials carousel lacks proper ARIA role and name

issuesFound.title: IconButton missing accessible name in ThemeToggle
issuesFound.explanation: The `<IconButton>` used in the ThemeToggle component renders only an icon (☀️/🌙/💻) and does not provide an accessible name. Screen‑reader users cannot know what the button does.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an `aria-label` that describes the action.  
Line 511: `<IconButton aria-label="Toggle theme" onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: IconButton missing accessible name in MobileNav close button
issuesFound.explanation: The close button inside MobileNav renders only the “✕” character without an accessible name, making it invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Provide an `aria-label`.  
Line 382: `<IconButton aria-label="Close mobile navigation" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: IconButton missing accessible name on carousel “previous” control
issuesFound.explanation: The previous‑slide button in the TestimonialsCarousel contains only the “‹” glyph and lacks an accessible name, so screen‑reader users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an `aria-label`.  
Line 724: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: IconButton missing accessible name on carousel “next” control
issuesFound.explanation: The next‑slide button in the TestimonialsCarousel contains only the “›” glyph and lacks an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an `aria-label`.  
Line 734: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: Clickable backdrop div missing role/button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop `<div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} />` is clickable with a mouse but is not focusable and has no role, preventing keyboard users from activating it.
issuesFound.fix:
Give it a button role, make it focusable, and add keyboard handling.  
Line 352: `<div role="button" tabIndex={0} className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){onClose();}} />`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button does not indicate its expanded/collapsed state to AT (`aria‑expanded` is absent).
issuesFound.fix:
Bind `aria-expanded` to the open state.  
Line 215: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria‑expanded (second dropdown)
issuesFound.explanation: Same issue for the second dropdown in DesktopNav.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-expanded={isOpen}` to the button.  
Line 235: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu button missing aria‑expanded (Solutions)
issuesFound.explanation: The submenu toggle button does not expose its expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-expanded` bound to `isExpanded`.  
Line 408: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav submenu button missing aria‑expanded (Resources)
issuesFound.explanation: Same missing state indication for the Resources submenu.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-expanded={isExpanded}`.  
Line 428: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle “Monthly” button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles a binary state but does not convey its pressed state to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `aria-pressed={value==='monthly'}`.  
Line 564: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value==='monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle “Annual” button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Same missing state indication for the annual option.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: Broken ARIA reference
issuesFound.explanation: The <button id="faq-2-question"> references "faq-2-answer" via aria-controls, but the id "faq-2-answer" is not present in the document (it should be "faq-2-answer" but the element has id="faq-2-answer" — this is a false positive due to inventory completeness). However, the <button id="faq-1-question"> has no aria-controls at all, but this is not a broken reference. The actual broken reference is in the FAQ section: the <button id="faq-2-question"> has aria-controls="faq-2-answer" but the referenced id is correct. The only broken reference is the <button id="faq-3-question"> which references "faq-3-answer" but the element has id="faq-3-answer" — this is a false positive. The only genuine broken reference is the <button id="faq-4-question"> which references "faq-4-answer" but the element has id="faq-4-answer" — this is a false positive. After Phase 1 inventory, no broken references exist. This issue is skipped per rule [viii].

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement

This violates WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) and 2.4.11 (Focus Appearance) as keyboard users lose all visual indication of focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 350: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator in the same rule. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible) rule
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus:not(:focus-visible) rule explicitly removes the focus outline for mouse users but does not provide a visible alternative for keyboard users who rely on focus indicators. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 309: :focus:not(:focus-visible) {
    Line 310: }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule inside the forced-colors media query sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This removes focus visibility for users in high-contrast mode, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm rule sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 (Target Size) minimum of 24px. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon rule sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24px. This makes the icon button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle rule sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 recommended size of 44px. While 28px meets the minimum, it may still be difficult for some users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button rules set min-height: 28px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 recommended size of 44px. While 28px meets the minimum, it may still be difficult for some users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a rule sets min-height: 28px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 recommended size of 44px. While 28px meets the minimum, it may still be difficult for some users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] rule sets width: 28px and height: 28px, both below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24px (but recommended 44px). This makes the search button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button rule sets min-height: 28px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 recommended size of 44px. While 28px meets the minimum, it may still be difficult for some users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button rule sets min-height: 28px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 recommended size of 44px. While 28px meets the minimum, it may still be difficult for some users to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .faq-item dt button
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button rule sets min-height: 60px, but the touch target area may be reduced by padding and surrounding elements. The effective touch target should be at least 44px × 44px, which is not guaranteed here.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img rule applies a transform animation on hover, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable or slow this motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card rule applies a transform animation on hover, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable or slow this motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card a:hover::after
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:hover::after rule applies a transform animation on hover, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable or slow this motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .product-card rule applies a transform animation on hover, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable or slow this motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card rule applies a transform animation on hover, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable or slow this motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured rule applies a transform animation on hover, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable or slow this motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item rule applies a transform animation on hover, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable or slow this motion. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions).

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by making text harder to parse.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The a rule sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from links. Since no other non-colour visual differentiator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom) is provided on :hover or :focus, links are distinguished from surrounding text only by colour, violating WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color).

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px on html rule
issuesFound.explanation: The html rule sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended minimum of 16px for body text. This can impair readability for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class incorrectly uses position: static, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) by making content unavailable to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers on focus
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only-focusable:focus and .sr-only-focusable:focus-within rules incorrectly use position: static, which does not restore content to the accessibility tree when focused. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) and 2.5.3 (Label in Name).

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle` function in `mobileNav` toggles the CSS class `is-open` on the drawer element but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, impacting users who rely on assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`) when the mobile navigation is closed on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the initial state is not programmatically determinable.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `toggle` function in `mobileNav` toggles the navigation menu state but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) as users who rely on assistive technologies are not informed of the change.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_validateAll` function in `contactForm` validates form fields and renders error messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as screen readers cannot programmatically determine which fields contain errors.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in `inlineValidator` removes error messages and error classes when a field is valid but does not clear the `aria-invalid` attribute. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 as the field remains marked as invalid even after correction.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `bind` function in `inlineValidator` does not set an initial `aria-invalid="false"` on form fields when the page loads. While this is not strictly required by WCAG, it is a best practice to ensure the attribute is present and reflects the initial valid state.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in `pricing` toggles the billing period but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the state is not programmatically determinable.

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `pricing` does not set an initial `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`) when the page loads. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the initial state is not programmatically determinable.

fpTitles: aria-invalid not initialised on page load

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` toggle the `is-open` class and update the DOM but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change of the navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-pressed on toggle button

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `mobileNav` change the state of the navigation menu but do not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers may not be aware of the state change, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in `inlineValidator` adds error classes and messages but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute to `true` on invalid fields. This prevents screen readers from announcing the error state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

    ```

    ```

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in `inlineValidator` does not clear the `aria-invalid` attribute when a field becomes valid. This can mislead screen reader users into thinking a field is still invalid, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13000: Add the following after `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`:
    ```
    setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load


issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `pricing` does not initialise the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`) to reflect the initial billing period. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as the initial state is not conveyed to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` trigger button (`_trigger`) has a click handler (`_onTriggerClick`) but lacks corresponding `keydown` handlers for `Enter` and `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1, as keyboard users cannot activate the navigation menu.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content injected via innerHTML without adjacent ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `render` function in `suggestionListRenderer` uses `innerHTML` to inject search suggestions but does not update an adjacent ARIA live region to announce the new content to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3, as dynamic changes are not announced.


issuesFound.title: setTimeout/setInterval changing visible content without pause/stop mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: The `_scheduleNext` function in `testimonialsCarousel` uses `setTimeout` to auto-advance slides but does not provide a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism. This violates WCAG 2.2.2, as users cannot control the timing of content changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11950: The existing `_playBtn` click handler (`togglePlay`) provides a pause/stop mechanism, so no additional fix is needed beyond ensuring the button is keyboard-accessible (already addressed in Issue 17).

issuesFound.title: focus() not managed on modal/dialog open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `mobileNav` does not programmatically move focus to the first focusable element inside the navigation menu when opened. This violates WCAG 2.4.3, as keyboard focus is not managed correctly for modal dialogs.

issuesFound.title: focus() not returned on modal/dialog close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in `mobileNav` does not return focus to the trigger button (`_trigger`) when the navigation menu is closed. This violates WCAG 2.4.3, as keyboard focus is not restored to the triggering element.
issuesFound.fix:


issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel dots in `testimonialsCarousel` are `<button>` elements but lack explicit `role="tab"` and corresponding keyboard navigation (arrow keys to move between tabs). This violates WCAG 2.1.1 and 4.1.2, as the role and keyboard operability are not correctly implemented.

issuesFound.title: Click handler on non-native element without matching keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The chip removal buttons in `activeFilterChips` have a click handler (`_onChipClick`) but lack corresponding `keydown` handlers for `Enter` and `Space` keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1, as keyboard users cannot remove filter chips.

issuesFound.title: aria-controls reference to non-existent element
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function in `dropdownMenu` sets `aria-controls` on the trigger button to reference the panel's ID, but the panel may not have an ID if it was not set in the HTML. This violates WCAG 1.3.1, as the `aria-controls` reference is broken.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on filter tabs without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Filter buttons do not show focus state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on pricing toggle without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Billing toggle buttons lack focus indicators.

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Text intended for screen readers only will be visible to all users, cluttering the interface.

issuesFound.title: Root font size set below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html element font-size is set to 12px. This scales all rem-based text (including body text) down to 12px, which is below the recommended 16px minimum for readability.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can cause letters to overlap or become illegible for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector uses transition: transform. The associated @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 743 is empty and does not disable this motion.
