issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple selectors set `outline: none` on `:focus-visible` without providing a visible alternative such as `box-shadow` or `border`. This removes the keyboard focus indicator, making it impossible for keyboard-only users to identify which element is active (WCAG 2.4.7 / 2.4.11).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` and `input[type="checkbox"]` have explicit dimensions of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24x24 CSS pixels (SC 2.5.8). This makes activation difficult for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 497: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }
Line 1249: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `clip: auto`, which fails to hide content visually. This exposes screen-reader-only text to sighted users, cluttering the interface, and may not hide it correctly from assistive technologies depending on the browser.
issuesFound.fix: Line 368: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: `.skip-link`, `.btn`, and `.hero-image img` use `transition: transform` which causes spatial movement. There is no effective `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override that disables these specific transitions, which can cause vestibular disorders for sensitive users (WCAG 2.3.3).

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text harder to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision (WCAG 1.4.12).
issuesFound.fix: Line 266: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-primary` uses `#5588bb` background and `#ffffff` text. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.4:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 473: .btn-primary { background-color: #004aaa; border-color: #004aaa; color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-danger` uses `#ee9999` background and `#ffffff` text. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.5:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (WCAG 1.4.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 507: .btn-danger { background-color: #991b1b; border-color: #991b1b; color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a` selector sets `text-decoration: none` without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as an underline on hover or focus, or a border) for all links. Users who cannot perceive colour differences may not identify links (WCAG 1.4.1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 273: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a visible alternative (such as outline-color, box-shadow, or border). This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements that do not have a more specific override, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on interactive components without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Specific interactive components (buttons, links, wrappers) explicitly set outline: none on :focus-visible but fail to provide a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This compounds the global focus removal, ensuring these elements remain invisible to keyboard users even if the global rule is fixed.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility implementation incorrect
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which does not hide content visually. Despite the comment claiming it is "visually hidden", elements with this class will be fully visible on the page, cluttering the interface for sighted users while failing to provide the intended screen-reader-only experience.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses the space between words. This reduces legibility, particularly for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision, and can cause words to visually merge.

issuesFound.title: Root font-size below 16px reduces readability
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px. Since body text uses 1rem (which is relative to html), all body text renders at 12px by default. This is below the recommended 16px minimum for body text, straining readability for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses a transform transition for its reveal animation. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (Line 453) is empty and does not disable this transition, causing unwanted motion for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 requires a minimum target size of 24x24 CSS pixels for interactive controls to ensure they can be activated by users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple text elements use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.85:1, which fails WCAG SC 1.4.3 (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text), making the text difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer and social links section have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover to indicate interactivity. This fails WCAG SC 1.4.1 (Use of Color), as color alone is not a robust indicator for users with color blindness.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button (_trigger) is managed by open() and close() functions that change the drawer's visibility classes but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed when interacting with the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function: mobileNav.open: add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after addClass(_drawer, ...).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not set initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button (_trigger) is initialized without an aria-expanded attribute, leaving its state undefined for assistive technologies on page load. Even if the menu is closed by default, the attribute must explicitly reflect this state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function: mobileNav.init: add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after _trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger buttons (inst.trigger) are toggled by _openInstance and _closeInstance functions that show/hide the panel but fail to update aria-expanded on the trigger. This prevents screen readers from announcing the correct state of the dropdown menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function: dropdownMenu._openInstance: add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not set initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown pair is registered, the trigger element (trigger) is not given an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute, even though the panel is hidden. Assistive technologies require this initial state to be explicit.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function: dropdownMenu.register: add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function updates the panel visibility and classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state management that confuses screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function: faqAccordion.open: add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after item.open = true;.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter tab buttons ([data-filter-value]) toggle their visual active class but fail to update the aria-pressed attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the state change is not conveyed to non-visual users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates: add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function associates an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field element. This prevents assistive technologies from identifying the field as having a validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function: srUtils.markInvalid: add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); before addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (set to "false" or removed) on the field. The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technologies even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Function: srUtils.markValid: add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); inside the if (isElement(errorEl)...) block.

fpTitles: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions modify CSS classes and scroll lock but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu instance open and close functions toggle the hidden attribute and CSS classes but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the inst.trigger button. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function sets the item state to open and animates the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the item.trigger. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix:
Function: faqAccordion.open
  setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing period toggle buttons switch visual active states via CSS classes but do not update the aria-pressed attribute. Screen readers will not identify which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Function: pricing._syncToggleState
  toggleAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true', 'false', isActive);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technology may not announce the field as invalid upon focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Function: srUtils.markInvalid
  field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility removes the error description but fails to remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field. The field may remain announced as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix:
Function: srUtils.markValid
  field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge text is updated dynamically when filters change, but no live region announcement is triggered. Screen reader users are not informed that the number of products has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Function: productFilter._updateCountBadge
  liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing plan card update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The price display text is updated dynamically when the billing period changes, but no live region announcement is triggered. Screen reader users are not informed that the price has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Function: pricing._updatePlanCard
  liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target}`);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.auditImages forces decorative alt text on all missing images
issuesFound.explanation: The auditImages function blindly sets alt="" on any image missing an alt attribute. This incorrectly marks informative images as decorative, hiding their purpose from screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Function: srUtils.auditImages if (!img.hasAttribute('alt')) { warn(`srUtils.auditImages: missing alt attribute`, img); img.setAttribute('alt', 'Image description missing'); }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.processSvgIcons hides functional unnamed SVGs
issuesFound.explanation: The processSvgIcons function sets aria-hidden="true" on any SVG without a name. If the SVG is a functional icon (e.g., inside a button without text), this hides the function from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Function: srUtils.processSvgIcons
  if (!hasName && !closest(svg, 'button, a')) { svg.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialization does not set initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization finds the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger. The initial state is ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Function: mobileNav.init
  setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu registration does not set initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu registration function sets aria-haspopup but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger. The initial state is ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Function: dropdownMenu.register
  setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

fpTitles: srUtils.auditImages forces decorative alt text on all missing images
dropdownMenu registration does not set initial aria-expanded state

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an aria-describedby reference for the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error, not just that it has a description. This blocks access to error state information for assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (e.g., by setting it to "false" or removing it). This leaves the field in a permanently "invalid" state in the accessibility tree even after the user corrects the error.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Assistive technology users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed when interacting with the toggle button.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and dropdownMenu._closeInstance functions show and hide the dropdown panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the state change is not communicated to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestionListRenderer does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.show and suggestionListRenderer.hide functions toggle the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the associated search input (_inputEl). Screen reader users will not know when the suggestion list is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function updates the CSS active class on filter tab buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. These buttons act as toggles for filtering, and their state must be conveyed via ARIA, not just CSS classes.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function updates the CSS active class on billing period buttons but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. Users of assistive technology will not be able to determine which billing period is currently selected.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close function correctly sets it to "false", the open function leaves the attribute stale, causing screen readers to announce the item as collapsed even when it is expanded.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField uses deprecated aria-dropeffect
issuesFound.explanation: The fileUploadField.bind function sets aria-dropeffect="none" on the upload zone. This attribute was deprecated in WAI-ARIA 1.1 and should not be used. It provides no benefit and may cause confusion in newer assistive technologies.

fpTitles: fileUploadField uses deprecated aria-dropeffect

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component renders a loading spinner when isLoading is true, but does not set aria-busy="true" on the underlying button element. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is busy processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 530: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? 'true' : undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} >

issuesFound.title: Spinner icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The inner span of the Spinner component (spinner__ring) is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". This may cause screen readers to announce irrelevant content or focus stops.
issuesFound.fix: Line 621: <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk indicating a required field is not hidden from assistive technology. It may be announced as "asterisk" or cause confusion without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 666: *

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not apply aria-invalid="true" to the input element when invalid. Screen readers cannot detect the error state programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 693: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Landmark region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop but fails to apply it to the rendered element. This prevents sections from having accessible names, which is required when multiple sections exist.
issuesFound.fix: Line 753: <Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} >

issuesFound.title: Repeated landmark region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a <nav> element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page (DesktopNav, MobileNav, Footer), this nav cannot be distinguished by screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 597: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links that represent the current page (item.current) lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users are not informed which link corresponds to the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 896: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} >

issuesFound.title: Icon-only interactive element missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used to close the mobile navigation menu does not have an aria-label prop. The button contains only an icon, making it inaccessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 933: <IconButton onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close navigation menu" className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: Decorative duplicate content not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The clone list in the IntegrationsBar marquee is not hidden from assistive technology. This causes screen readers to announce the list of integrations twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1332: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: Interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing plan list item has a visual badge but no programmatic indication (aria-label) that it is featured/recommended. Screen reader users miss this distinction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650: <li aria-label={plan.featured ? `${plan.name} plan (Most popular)` : undefined} className={['pricing-card', plan.featured ? 'pricing-card--featured' : '', isSelected ? 'pricing-card--selected' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} >

issuesFound.title: Hidden slide content not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: Slides in the testimonials carousel that are not visible lack aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce all slides, not just the active one.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1773: <div role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} >

issuesFound.title: aria-modal placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the element with role="dialog". This prevents assistive technologies from correctly identifying the modal boundary.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or border-color change. This removes the default browser focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 350: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, outline: none is applied to :focus-visible without a replacement visible indicator. In high contrast mode, this leaves users unable to track focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 360: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: .btn:focus-visible removes outline without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change, making button focus invisible for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The open() and close() functions toggle the mobile navigation drawer visibility by adding/removing CSS classes, but neither updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1527: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Line 1540: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the module initialises. The ARIA state must reflect the initial closed state of the drawer on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1568 (after `_initialised = true;`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open does not move focus into drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or its first focusable element. Keyboard users cannot perceive the new content without manually navigating into it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1529 (after `_lockScroll();`): Add `if (_drawer) { const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true }); }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the focus order and must navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1540 (after `_unlockScroll();`): Add `if (_trigger) { _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true }); }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated on open
issuesFound.explanation: The focus trap instance (_trap) is created but never activated when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background page content, violating the modal focus containment requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1529 (after `_lockScroll();`): Add `if (_trap) { _trap.activate(); }`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide dropdown panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger) to reflect the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2093 (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line 2104 (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are not initialised with aria-expanded="false" to match the initially hidden state of their panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2107 (after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands the FAQ panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close() function correctly sets it to "false", the asymmetry means screen readers never hear the expanded state announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2744 (after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (_periodBtns) act as a two-state toggle (month vs annual) but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to indicate which button is currently active/pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2308 (inside forEach loop): Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the validator marks the field invalid via CSS and aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true". When validation passes, it does not clear aria-invalid (set to "false" or remove it). Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1970 (after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`
  Line 1973 (after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: toastManager auto-dismisses without user control mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a timeout (line 1332) without providing a mechanism for the user to pause, stop, or hide the notification. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable for auto-updating content.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the change in result count is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the content has updated or how many products are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2183 (after `_syncPagination(totalMatches);`): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` (or use the existing STRINGS.filterApplied message)

fpTitles: mobileNav open does not move focus into drawer
dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
toastManager auto-dismisses without user control mechanism

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens and closes by toggling CSS classes, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the visible state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1621: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line 1641: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the code animates the panel and updates classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (the close function correctly sets it to false, creating an asymmetry).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2677: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function marks a field as invalid by linking an error message via aria-describedby, but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the input itself. Screen readers rely on this attribute to identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1416: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes the error message from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1440: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`)

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge text is updated visually when filters are applied, but the change is not announced via a live region, so screen reader users are unaware of the updated result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2327: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) visually indicate the active state via CSS classes, but do not expose the pressed state to assistive technologies using aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2539: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, the trigger button does not have aria-expanded set to "false" to indicate the menu is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1687: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown pair, the trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false", leaving it without a state declaration until first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons are not initialized with aria-pressed on page load, failing to indicate which period is active before user interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2599: After `setPeriod(initPeriod, false);`, add:
  `qsa('[data-period]').forEach(btn => setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(btn.getAttribute('data-period') === initPeriod)));`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox with a suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is never set to true when the list opens or false when it closes, violating the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2139: Add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (initial state)
  Line 2204: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line 2215: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons (acting as toggle buttons) update their visual active state but do not set aria-pressed to communicate the active state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2361: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: A focus trap is created (createFocusTrap) and stored in `_trap`, but the `open` function never calls `_trap.activate()`, meaning keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer when it opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1627: Add `_trap.activate();` (after `_lockScroll();`)

fpTitles: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button is created in the closed state on page load, but the JavaScript initialization code does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element. Screen readers will not announce the collapsed state of the menu button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3140: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_initialised = true;` in the init function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger not updating aria-expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened or closed via the open() and close() functions, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the current visibility state. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3080: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of the open function before the emit call. Line 3100: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of the close function before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers not updating aria-expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show and hide the dropdown panels but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons to reflect the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3220: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in _openInstance after setting inst.open = true. Line 3230: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in _closeInstance after setting inst.open = false.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox (role="combobox", aria-haspopup="listbox") but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" when the page loads with the suggestion list closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4200: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after setting the role and aria-haspopup attributes in the init function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox not updating aria-expanded on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion combobox opens and closes dynamically, but the aria-expanded attribute on the input element is never updated to reflect whether the suggestion listbox is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4080: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of the _open function. Line 4090: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of the _close function.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) are not initialized with aria-pressed to indicate which period is active on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5240: After calling setPeriod(initPeriod, false), iterate over _periodBtns and set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-period') === initPeriod));`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons not updating aria-pressed on selection
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the _syncToggleState function updates CSS classes but does not update the aria-pressed attribute on the period buttons to indicate the active selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5210: Inside the forEach loop over _periodBtns, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after calculating isActive.

issuesFound.title: pricing switch element not updating aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period switch element (_toggleEl) has role="switch" but its aria-checked attribute is never updated when the period toggles between month and year.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5210: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));` after calculating isYear.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the _renderResult function adds error classes and messages but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. When validation passes, it does not clear aria-invalid to "false".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~6100 (in the else branch for invalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. In the if branch for valid: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product count badge updates, the new count is only written to a visible element and its aria-label, but no announcement is made via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the filter results have changed or how many items match.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4620: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);` after updating the badge textContent.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually with animation, but the change in billing period and prices is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5200: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (or monthly equivalent based on the period parameter) after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus is moved, but no confirmation is announced to screen readers that the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3380: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');` after the scrollToY call in the _onClick function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter category tabs not updating aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter category tabs are implemented as buttons that toggle the active filter, but when the active category changes, the aria-pressed attribute is not updated to reflect which tab is currently pressed/selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4680: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after calculating isActive.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1020-1022: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ accordion buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ item buttons' focus-visible rule removes outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which FAQ question has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1117: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox inputs' focus-visible rule removes outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when a checkbox has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1162-1163: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer brand links' focus-visible rule removes outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when these links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1180-1182: .footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links' focus-visible rule removes outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when footer nav links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185-1187: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The social media links' focus-visible rule removes outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when social links have focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1195-1197: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) rule removes outline on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. In Windows High Contrast mode, this removes the only focus indicator available, making keyboard navigation impossible for users who rely on this mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 234-241: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid Highlight; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes content FULLY VISIBLE instead of visually hidden. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only pattern, causing content meant to be hidden from sighted users to remain visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 213-222: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Search input label hidden from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The rule #site-search label.sr-only { display: none; } hides the search input's label from all users, including screen readers. Form inputs must have accessible labels. Since the .sr-only class is already broken, this display: none rule ensures the label is completely inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 439-441: #site-search label.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on a gradient background (#5588bb to #bb88ee). The resulting contrast ratio is approximately 1.5:1, far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision cannot read these labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 917-918: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments or using touch devices may have difficulty activating these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 352-356: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon-only buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the WCAG 2.2 minimum of 24px. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult to activate with touch when undersized.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363-366: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox inputs are sized at 20px × 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target. Users with motor impairments may struggle to check boxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1160-1161: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on default buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, below the recommended 44px touch target for comfortable activation. While above the 24px minimum, this may still cause difficulty for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 311: .btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5);

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on large buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class sets min-height: 36px, below the recommended 44px touch target. Large/primary CTAs should meet or exceed 44px for optimal accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 358-361: .btn-lg { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); font-size: var(--font-size-lg); border-radius: var(--radius-xl); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on button active state
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active rule applies transform: translateY(1px) without a prefers-reduced-motion override. Users who have motion sensitivity preferences enabled will still experience this movement animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 325: .btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform and transition properties for a 3D perspective effect, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 592-597 is empty and does not disable the animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 592-597: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover rule applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01). The existing reduced-motion override only targets .pricing-card:hover, not the more specific .pricing-card--featured:hover, so the animation persists for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 969-972: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses the space between words. Negative word-spacing can impair readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision by making text appear cramped.
issuesFound.fix: Line 183: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links globally. Without underlines, links must be distinguished from surrounding text by more than color alone (WCAG 1.4.1). The stylesheet does not provide an alternative non-color visual indicator for links in body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 167: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add: .prose a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Root font-size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) default. This makes all rem-based text smaller than optimal for readability, particularly affecting users with low vision or reading difficulties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 179: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 16px; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Line-height below minimum for paragraph text
issuesFound.explanation: The p selector sets line-height: 1, which is far below the recommended 1.5 minimum for body text. This severely impacts readability by crowding lines of text together, making it difficult for users with dyslexia or low vision to track lines.
issuesFound.fix: Line 206: p { line-height: 1.5; text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is completely broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is supposed to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. Instead, this implementation does the opposite — it makes content fully visible with position: static, width: auto, and overflow: visible. Any element using this class will not be hidden at all, breaking skip links, screen reader text, and other accessibility patterns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 287-296: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non-colour alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes link underlines without providing a non-colour visual differentiator. This violates SC 1.4.1 as colour alone is used to distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 226: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add: .prose a, article a, main a { text-decoration: underline; text-underline-offset: 2px; }

fpTitles: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
Motion animation with empty prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function opens a dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but never updates aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the dropdown is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1953: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function closes a dropdown panel but never updates aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the dropdown is now collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1960: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links an error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot announce that the field contains invalid input per WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes error descriptions but never clears aria-invalid. Fields remain marked as invalid even after corrections are made.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1251: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after `if (!isElement(field)) return;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but never sets the initial aria-expanded="false" state. The trigger button lacks the required ARIA attribute on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2055: add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialisation does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The ARIA state is absent until the first interaction, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1901: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function only toggles CSS classes on filter tabs, not aria-pressed. Filter tabs acting as toggle buttons must update aria-pressed to indicate their active/inactive state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2618: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function only toggles CSS classes on billing period buttons, not aria-pressed. Toggle buttons must update aria-pressed to indicate their selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2795: add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle element receives role="switch" but aria-checked is never updated when the period changes. A switch role requires aria-checked to reflect its state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2800: add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));` after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter results count not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count changes but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the results count change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2588: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);` inside the onDone callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never updates aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2108 (after addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);): add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function removes CSS classes to hide the navigation drawer but never updates aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the collapsed state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2122 (after _open = false;): add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function sets item.open to true and adds CSS classes but never calls setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'). The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded to false, creating an inconsistent state where screen readers cannot announce the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4103 (after item.open = true;): add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function only links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1502 (after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);): add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function manages aria-describedby but never clears aria-invalid when the field becomes valid. The invalid state persists in the accessibility tree even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1519 (at end of function, before closing brace): add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function sets up event listeners but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button at initialization. The ARIA state is absent until the first interaction, violating WCAG 4.1.2 which requires state attributes reflect current state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2185 (after _initialised = true;): add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialise aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set on page load. The initial state must be declared so assistive technologies can convey it before the first user interaction, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1755: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not set initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, aria-expanded="false" is not set initially. The initial collapsed state must be declared so assistive technologies can convey it before the first user interaction, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1525: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the line that sets aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing toggle element has role="switch" set, aria-checked is not updated to reflect the on/off state. For role="switch", aria-checked is required to convey the current state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2755: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));` after the toggleClass call for the toggle track.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the visible product count changes, the update is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users cannot perceive that the content has changed, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2285: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || STRINGS.announce.filterAll);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the update is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users cannot perceive that the pricing display has changed, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2705: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component accepts an htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the <label> element. Without htmlFor, screen readers cannot associate the label with its form control, violating WCAG 1.3.1 and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 452: <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label"> {

issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot announce the field's error state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 472: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot announce the field's error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 490: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Select missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen readers cannot announce the field's error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 518: <select id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-select', invalid ? 'form-select--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} >

issuesFound.fix: Line 538: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle} >

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal placed on wrong element
